ddls:
CREATE TABLE "holidays" (
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday */,
  "day" TEXT /* Day of the week the holiday falls on */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the holiday in format 'MMM DD' */,
  "holiday_name" TEXT /* Official name of the holiday */,
  "holiday_type" TEXT /* Type of holiday (e.g., National Holiday) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or descriptions regarding the holiday */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the holiday */,
  "is_fixed" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the date is fixed or varies */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the holiday entry */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the holiday entry was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the holiday entry */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the holiday entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holiday_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the holiday type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the holiday type */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the holiday type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the holiday type was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the holiday type */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the holiday type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Code representing the region (e.g., US for United States) */,
  "parent_region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent region */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the region */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the region was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the region */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holiday_region_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping record */,
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated holiday */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_holiday_region_mapping_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_holiday_region_mapping_holiday_id FOREIGN KEY ("holiday_id") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated holiday */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., parade, concert) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the event is held */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the event */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the event */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the event */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the event */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_holiday_id FOREIGN KEY ("holiday_id") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., citizen, government official) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* User's phone number for notifications */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* ID of the holiday associated with the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_holiday_id FOREIGN KEY ("holiday_id") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holiday_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* ID of the holiday being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Content of the comment */,
  "is_approved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the comment is approved */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the comment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_holiday_comments_holiday_id FOREIGN KEY ("holiday_id") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_holiday_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_holidays" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_holiday_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping record */,
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated holiday */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_holiday_mapping_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES country_holidays ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_holiday_mapping_holiday_id FOREIGN KEY ("holiday_id") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id")
)
